01_initial_request.input_data: Wild type (WT) and R337H mutant p53TD tetramer structural models
01_initial_request.user_intent: To understand how the cancer-related R337H mutation reduces the structural stability of the p53TD tetramer
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Structural stability of the p53TD tetramer

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Wild type (WT) p53TD tetramer structure is available from PDB ID 2J0Z. [Gap] A structural model of the cancer-related R337H mutant is required for comparative simulation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of comparing WT and mutant stability, chose to use Pymol to perform site-directed mutagenesis on the WT structure, expecting to obtain the R337H p53TD tetramer structural model.

parameters.operation: replace Arg (R) at position 337 with His (H)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: R337H p53TD tetramer structural model generated with capped termini.

references.citation: W. L. Delano, The PyMOL Molecular Graphics System Version 2.4.1, Schrodinger LLC, 2021 (Ref 67)
references.relevance: Software used for generating the mutant structure and visualization.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] WT and R337H structural models are prepared. [Gap] Dynamic stability and conformational properties need to be sampled over time. [Decision] To achieve the goal of understanding structural stability, chose to use GROMACS to perform all-atom explicit-solvent MD simulations on both systems, expecting to obtain microsecond-scale trajectories.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Accumulated 20 μs simulation trajectories (10 μs per system) generated for WT and R337H tetramers.

references.citation: M. J. Abraham et al., SoftwareX, 2015 (Ref 68)
J. Huang et al., Nat. Methods, 2017 (Ref 69)
references.relevance: Force field used for protein simulations.

references.citation: H. J. C. Berendsen et al., Intermol. Forces, 1981 (Ref 70)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MD trajectories are available for both systems. [Gap] Quantitative metrics of structural stability (RMSD, RMSF, Rg, SASA, H-bonds) need to be calculated. [Decision] To achieve the goal of quantifying stability, chose to use GROMACS tools to perform trajectory analysis on the protein conformations, expecting to obtain time-evolution profiles of structural metrics.

parameters.metrics: RMSD, RMSF, Rg, SASA, H-bond number

02_agent_trajectory.observation: R337H mutant showed larger RMSD (0.34 nm vs 0.26 nm), fewer H-bonds (100 vs 115), and increased SASA compared to WT.

references.citation: M. J. Abraham et al., SoftwareX, 2015 (Ref 68)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] General stability metrics indicate destabilization. [Gap] Specific residue-residue interactions driving destabilization need to be identified. [Decision] To achieve the goal of mechanistic insight, chose to use In-house codes to calculate pairwise residue contact numbers, expecting to obtain contact maps highlighting disrupted interactions.

parameters.atomic_contact_definition: 0.54 nm for carbon atoms, 0.46 nm for other atoms

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Reduced contacts observed for R337-M340, F338-R342, and R337-D352 pairs in the mutant.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Contact analysis suggests secondary structure changes. [Gap] The extent of secondary structure unfolding needs to be quantified. [Decision] To achieve the goal of characterizing structural changes, chose to use DSSP to calculate secondary structure probabilities, expecting to obtain helix and sheet content over time.

parameters.output: residue-based probability of β-sheet and α-helix

02_agent_trajectory.observation: α-helix probability decreased by 5.0% in mutant; N-terminal region (335-340) unfolding observed.

references.citation: W. Kabsch and C. Sander, Biopolymers, 1983 (Ref 77)
references.relevance: Algorithm used for secondary structure calculation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Structural metrics show conformational shifts. [Gap] The global conformational distribution and energy landscape need to be mapped. [Decision] To achieve the goal of understanding conformational ensemble, chose to use In-house codes to perform PCA and construct Free Energy Landscape, expecting to obtain energy minima locations.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: WT showed single free-energy minimum; Mutant showed three minima shifted towards disordered states.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Conformational changes are identified. [Gap] The dynamical correlation and connectivity between residues need to be analyzed. [Decision] To achieve the goal of understanding allosteric effects, chose to use Fargle and Sethi codes to construct dynamical networks and perform community analysis, expecting to obtain community connectivity maps.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutant showed reduced dynamical correlation and broken connectivity between communities (e.g., community-1 and 2).

references.citation: A. Sethia et al., Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U. S. A., 2009 (Ref 81)
references.relevance: Codes used for dynamical network analysis.

references.citation: M. Girvan and M. E. J. Newman, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U. S. A., 2002 (Ref 82)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] All computational analyses are complete. [Gap] Structural changes need to be visually represented for interpretation. [Decision] To achieve the goal of presenting results, chose to use Pymol to perform graphical analysis and structural visualization, expecting to obtain representative snapshots.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Snapshots confirmed disruption of HP cores and unfolding of α-helix in R337H mutant.

references.citation: W. L. Delano, The PyMOL Molecular Graphics System Version 2.4.1, Schrodinger LLC, 2021 (Ref 67)

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Molecular Dynamics Simulation and Community Network Analysis

RMSD.interpretation: Higher RMSD in mutant indicates reduced structural stability.

H-bond Number.interpretation: Fewer H-bonds in mutant indicate weakened intramolecular interactions.

Alpha-helix Probability.interpretation: Decreased helix probability confirms unfolding of N-terminal α-helix region.

Free Energy Minima.interpretation: Multiple minima in mutant indicate shift towards disordered conformational states.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully elucidated the atomistic mechanism by which the R337H mutation destabilizes the p53TD tetramer through the disruption of specific salt bridges, hydrophobic interactions, and π-π stacking, leading to α-helix unfolding and reduced global connectivity.
